justification: All 5+ rules identified (4 invariant: true, 3 invariant: false for weight brackets). State machine directionality explicitly modeled with in_transit -> pending forbidden (BR-2) and delivered terminal (BR-3). 13 acceptance_criteria all in Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Weight bracket rules correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification. Only minor issue: confidence level of 92 exceeds the 65-90 ideal range, but assumptions are thoroughly listed including the Pending->Delivered skip inference.

observations.text: Exceeded minimum rule count by splitting weight brackets into 3 separate rules (BR-5, BR-6, BR-7), each with specific boundary test values
State machine directionality is explicit: BR-2 names backward transitions as forbidden, BR-3 names Delivered as terminal
All 13 acceptance criteria use strict Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions
Testable assertions are specific (e.g., 'weight 9.99 kg must be classified as Standard') rather than vague
Edge cases section covers boundary values, floating-point precision, empty-string addresses, and skip-state transitions
Invariant justifications are well-reasoned, distinguishing physical constraints from business policy
Confidence level of 92 slightly exceeds the 65-90 rubric range; given the noted assumptions (Pending->Delivered skip, address format ambiguity, weight mutability), 85-88 would be more calibrated
AC-13 proactively covers the Pending->Delivered skip case, which was also flagged as a low-confidence inference — good self-awareness

grader_results.detail: field confidence.level value 92 is above max 90
